item.title: IFM Investors Raises Price in Atlas Arteria Takeover Bid
item.description: IFM Investors has increased its bid and removed certain conditions in its best and final offer for Australian-listed toll road operator Atlas Arteria Ltd.

item.title: Indonesia Awaits MSCI Verdict That Risks $13 Billion Outflows
item.description: Indonesia faces a crucial test this month when MSCI Inc. decides whether to follow through with a downgrade, with investors already questioning the resilience of the world’s worst-performing equity market.

item.title: Yen Short Bets Jump to Nine-Year High as Carry Trade Revives
item.description: Speculators have boosted their bets against the yen to a nine-year high, signaling the revival of the yen carry trade despite intervention risks and a potential rate hike by the Bank of Japan on Tuesday.

item.title: South Korea’s Floundering Movie Business Turns to AI for Help
item.description: Two films built largely with generative AI tools are on view in South Korean theaters

item.title: BOJ Set to Hike Rates to Highest Since 1995 Despite Ueda Absence
item.description: The Bank of Japan is widely expected to raise its benchmark interest rate to the highest level since 1995 at the first regular policy meeting ever held without the governor in attendance.

item.title: Japanese Stocks Set to Rise After US-Iran Peace Deal Reached
item.description: Japanese stocks are set to gain after President Donald Trump said the US and Iran have reached a peace deal, raising hopes the Middle East war is nearing an end and the Strait of Hormuz will reopen.

item.title: UK, France, Germany Ready to Lift Relevant Iran Sanctions
item.description: “We are prepared to lift relevant sanctions in response to clear, verifiable steps by Iran on its nuclear programme,” according to a joint statement from the leaders of the UK, France, Germany and Italy.

item.title: LNG Tanker Heads Toward Hormuz as Deal Raises Hopes of Reopening
item.description: A liquefied natural gas tanker that has been trapped in the Persian Gulf for over three months appears to be heading toward the Strait of Hormuz, just as the US and Iran say they have reached a deal to reopen the waterway.

item.title: Trump Says Hormuz Strait to Open Friday Upon Deal Signing
item.description: President Trump says the Strait of Hormuz will be “opening” on Friday upon the signing of the deal with Iran.

item.title: ASIC Says ASX Agrees to Pay Fine Over Misleading Chess Statement
item.description: Australia’s securities regulator said ASX Ltd. has admitted that its Feb. 10, 2022 market announcement which stated that the Chess replacement project was “progressing well” was misleading.

item.title: US Futures Climb, Oil Falls on Iran Peace Deal
item.description: US equity futures climbed, oil slumped and the dollar fell after President Donald Trump and Pakistani mediators said the US and Iran had reached a peace agreement that would reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Bailey Defends BOE Gilt Sales After Criticism From Farage
item.description: Governor Andrew Bailey defended the Bank of England’s buying and selling of UK government bonds following criticism of the policy, including Nigel Farage’s populist Reform UK party.

item.title: Oil Slumps as Trump Says Iran Deal Agreed and Hormuz to Reopen
item.description: Oil dropped after President Donald Trump said a deal with Iran was complete, potentially allowing the Strait of Hormuz to reopen.

item.title: Stock Market Moves On From SpaceX To Focus On Iran Deal, Fed
item.description: After SpaceX and its bankers pulled off the largest-ever IPO without a hitch last week, investors are returning their focus back to Earth.

item.title: Chinese Economy Stuck in Slow Lane as Consumption Heads for Drop
item.description: China’s consumer spending may have contracted for the first time since the pandemic, a setback that would extend a slowdown in an economy whose momentum is faltering despite booming trade.

item.title: Japan’s Top Bond-Trading Regional Bank Buys JGBs After Decade
item.description: The Japanese regional bank with the best recent record for trading bonds has started buying Japanese government debt for the first time in a decade.

item.title: Hong Kong Issuers Seek to Boost Trading in Overlooked Stocks
item.description: Hong Kong stock issuers are looking to boost trading in some stocks suffering from thin volumes, which they say is hurting the ability of companies to access financing through follow-on offerings.

item.title: Bond Funds Chase Australian Debt on Peak RBA Interest Rate Bets
item.description: Local and global bond funds are snapping up Australian debt as economic strains signal the end of the Reserve Bank’s hiking cycle.

item.title: RBA Set for First Rate Pause of 2026 as Bets on Further Hike Ebb
item.description: Australia is set to keep its key interest rate unchanged for the first time this year, with money markets paring bets on further tightening amid signs the economy is beginning to soften.

item.title: Global Funds Retreat From Japan’s Long Bonds as BOJ Goes Slow
item.description: Little more than a year since Japan finally offered yields high enough to lure global bond managers back to its debt market, many are starting to retreat.

item.title: SpaceX and OpenAI Are Ending Wall Street’s Era of Stock Scarcity
item.description: The stock market is starting to expand again after years of shrinking — and potentially at a scale not seen since the dot-com era.

item.title: Carney Says Anthropic Ban Shows Risk of Relying on Big AI Models
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney said the US export ban blocking all foreign access to Anthropic PBC’s latest artificial intelligence models underscores the risk of depending on just a handful of powerful AI tools.

item.title: Warsh Caught Between Trump and Bond Market Betting on Rate Hikes
item.description: Just three weeks into the job, Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh is already facing an unusually high-stakes test.

item.title: IDC, Vision Near Deal to Save Century-Old African Sugar Mills
item.description: Tycoon-backed Vision Group is in talks with South Africa’s Industrial Development Corp. to take an equity stake in sugar refiner Tongaat Hulett in exchange for additional funding, Vision Group shareholder and consortium member Robert Gumede said in an interview.

item.title: Apple’s New Siri Is Just Good Enough to Ease Its AI Crisis
item.description: The company prepares for the foldable iPhone and touch-screen MacBook.

item.title: Equity Funding Squeeze Driven By AI Rally, SpaceX IPO Stretches Banks
item.description: The cost to fund US equity positions is surging unexpectedly, as a combination of the AI-driven stock rally, the massive growth of leveraged ETFs and SpaceX’s $75 billion public listing tests Wall Street’s market plumbing.

item.title: Lazard Undercuts Rival Centerview in Battle for Venezuela Deal
item.description: Investment bank Lazard Inc. is making a late bid to dislodge rival Centerview Partners as Venezuela’s financial adviser, offering to oversee one of the largest-ever sovereign debt restructurings for a sharply lower fee.

item.title: Crypto Token’s 50% Wipeout Shows Magnitude of AI-Hacking Threat
item.description: When Eli Ben-Sasson helped create the Zcash cryptocurrency nearly a decade ago, the cryptographer worried about human adversaries. He didn’t expect that machine intelligence would one day expose a flaw that had eluded years of expert human judgment.

item.title: Diverging Rate Paths Force Reshuffle in Emerging-Market Bets
item.description: A growing divergence in interest-rate outlooks is forcing emerging-market investors to reshuffle bets.

item.title: AI Turns Coders, Lawyers and Analysts Into Ghosts of London’s Past
item.description: Search for “finance analyst” and you’re likely to find around 80 vacancies in London, according to a prominent recruitment website. If that seems like a surprisingly low number for one of the world’s biggest banking and corporate hubs, it’s because it is — four years earlier, the same website featured more than 350 of these roles.
